source_pr.title: executor: fix unstable TestCoprocessorOOMAction
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #19677 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

For asserting OOMAction in `TestCoprocessorOOMAction`, the testcase for the non keep-order is unstable as the `quota` might be not enough to support the whole query processing.

What's Changed:
Mock the consume data to make `TestCoprocessorOOMAction` stable.

How it Works:

- Unit test

Side effects

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

* No release note

symptoms: `TestCoprocessorOOMAction` is unstable because the memory quota boundary is sensitive to asynchronous coprocessor response sizes/ordering.
failure_signature: Non-deterministic OOM assertion: query may or may not hit `Out Of Memory Quota` depending on coprocessor response sizes/arrival order
root_cause_explanation: `TestCoprocessorOOMAction` asserts the OOM action by setting a query memory quota and expecting `Out Of Memory Quota` to be triggered. The non-keep-order case was unstable because coprocessor response sizes and scheduling are asynchronous, so the same quota can be exceeded at different times (or not exceeded deterministically), leading to flaky outcomes. This PR stabilizes the test by enabling a failpoint to mock per-response memory consumption to a deterministic value and by tuning the quota boundary so OOM is triggered consistently before asserting the error.
fix_pattern: Make quota/consumption tests deterministic: mock coprocessor memory consumption via failpoints and tune the quota threshold so the OOM boundary is crossed consistently.

evidence: evidence: PR title: executor: fix unstable TestCoprocessorOOMAction
evidence: add failpoint `testRateLimitActionMockConsume` to mock mem-tracker consumption + tune quota to deterministic values (executor/executor_test.go, store/tikv/coprocessor.go)
